generatedSentence: Elara, a kindhearted former classmate, often found herself sipping tea in her workshop, nostalgic for a simpler time. Her joysticks, originally used for games, now controlled the holographic disk orbiting her desk. She learned the folkways of her people, but felt increasingly marginal, left behind. A fuzzy definition of her own future kept her susceptible to anxiety; her cortisol levels were always humming. She meticulously shuffled data, often picking at a loose toenail, her caretaking duties for the lighthouse far away felt like a slow withdrawal from the world. The warlords' absolute jurisdictions had restricted trade, their ministers endorsing a brutal taxation that made life unbearable. A refugee, seeking asylum, became a plaintiff against the corrupt system, his trajectory leading him to Elara. She had a hunchbacks, an incognito informant, who helped her realise the true implication: powerful oppositions were using the crisis to instigate higher ventures. Elara, a talented organizer, knew she must undertake a supplementary mission, willing to confront the system. She possessed an inner chakra, her guiding arrow. As workdays passed, Elara acquired various materials and a palladium casing for a small grenade. The tabloid media, under surveillance, started publishing stories about a new hero. She jaywalked past old, crumbling gateways, where she learned of historical folkways that once bound communities. Like a jazz tune, her plan began to swirl, a camouflage against the clingy grip of fear. She remembered her old classroom, a chalk memory, feeling the girth of her responsibility, knowing she could not hesitate to protect the lives of those she sought to free. She vanquished the corrupt ministers, who glowered, their futures now bleak. The original slaves, now free citizens, saw their naturalization begin, their chains broken by Elara’s bold ventures. She left behind a keepsake of justice, a skullcap for a fallen comrade, a symbol of their collective fight. The grassland outside the city, where the kinkajou once roamed, felt hopeful. Elara, having received a long massage and a ladyfinger, became a graduate of a new era, proving that even a single individual, armed with scientific resolve, could redefine destinies.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist wears a deep indigo technician's jumpsuit with subtle glowing circuit patterns and a singular, sharp metallic silver cybernetic arm. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara, viewed from a medium shot, sips tea at a cluttered futuristic desk, holographic joysticks and a glowing data disk hovering, a distant, stylized lighthouse visible through a window. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, from a low-angle shot, stands resolute, a symbolic arrow glowing near her chest (chakra energy), confronting a shadowy group of robed figures (ministers) with a terrified refugee looking on. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A close-up on Elara's hands, carefully assembling a sleek palladium device (grenade), with ghostly chalk outlines of a classroom and ancient gateways faintly visible in the background. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot shows Elara, victorious and silhouetted against a sunrise over a vast grassland, the vanquished ministers in chains behind her, former slaves celebrating their freedom.

definitions.definition: A drug smoked or ingested for euphoric effect, cannabis.
definitions.example: After smoking a bowl of that fine marijuana, they ate some brownies.

definitions.definition: The hemp plant itself, Cannabis sativa.
Food consumed before going to bed.
Any meal eaten in the evening; dinner eaten in the evening, rather than at noon.
A meal from a chip shop consisting of a deep-fried food with chips.
definitions.example: a fish supper; a pizza supper

definitions.definition: A drinker, especially one who drinks slowly (i.e., one who sups).
The tea plant (Camellia sinensis); a variety of this plant.
definitions.example: Darjeeling tea is grown in India.

definitions.definition: The dried leaves or buds of the tea plant; a variety of such leaves.
definitions.example: Go to the supermarket and buy some Darjeeling tea.

definitions.definition: The drink made by infusing these dried leaves or buds in hot water.
definitions.example: Would you like some tea?

definitions.definition: Any similar drink made by infusing parts of various other plants.
definitions.example: camomile tea; mint tea

definitions.definition: Meat stock served as a hot drink.
definitions.example: beef tea

definitions.definition: A cup or (East Asia) glass of any of these drinks, often with milk, sugar, lemon, and/or tapioca pearls.
A light midafternoon meal, typically but not necessarily including tea.
The break in play between the second and third sessions.
definitions.example: Australia were 490 for 7 at tea on the second day.

definitions.definition: Information, especially gossip.
definitions.example: Spill the tea on that drama, hon.

definitions.definition: To drink tea.
To take afternoon tea (the light meal).
The power, right, or authority to interpret and apply the law.
The power or right to exercise authority.
The power or right to perform some action as part of applying the law.
The authority of a sovereign power to govern or legislate.
The limits or territory within which authority may be exercised.
A mechanical device consisting of a handgrip mounted on a base or pedestal and typically having one or more buttons, used to control an aircraft, computer or other equipment.
A penis.
A student who is in the same class at school.
definitions.example: Danny and I have been classmates for five years, but I've never spoken to him outside of lessons.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A member of a different sort of class, such as locomotives etc.
(Ayurveda) Any of (at least more than) seven centres of spiritual energy in the body, according to Ayurveda philosophy.
The steroid hormone hydrocortisone.
A person who is vulnerable to being infected by a certain disease
Likely to be affected by something
definitions.example: He was susceptible to minor ailments.

definitions.definition: Easily influenced or tricked; credulous
Especially sensitive, especially to a stimulus
That, when subjected to a specific operation, will yield a specific result
definitions.example: A properly prepared surface is susceptible of an enduring paint job.

definitions.definition: Vulnerable; (temporarily) defenseless
To put in a random order.
definitions.example: Don't forget to shuffle the cards.

definitions.definition: To change; modify the order of something.
To move in a slovenly, dragging manner; to drag or scrape the feet in walking or dancing.
definitions.example: He shuffled out of the room.

definitions.definition: To change one's position; to shift ground; to evade questions; to resort to equivocation; to prevaricate.
To use arts or expedients; to make shift.
To shove one way and the other; to push from one to another.
definitions.example: to shuffle money from hand to hand

definitions.definition: To remove or introduce by artificial confusion.
Jumbled together.
A statement of the meaning of a word or word group or a sign or symbol (dictionary definitions).
(usually with the definite article the) A clear instance conforming to the dictionary or textbook definition.
A statement expressing the essential nature of something; formulation
The action or process of defining.
The act of defining; determination of the limits.
A product of defining.
The action or power of describing, explaining, or making definite and clear.
definitions.example: Her comic genius is beyond definition.

definitions.definition: Clarity of visual presentation, distinctness of outline or detail.
definitions.example: Improve the definition of an image.

definitions.definition: Clarity, especially of musical sound in reproduction.
Sharp demarcation of outlines or limits.
definitions.example: A jacket with distinct waist definition.

definitions.definition: The degree to which individual muscles are distinct on the body.
A statement which provides a previous declaration with a value or body of a subroutine (in the case of function).
A statement that establishes the referent of a term or notation.
A person who is trained to preach, to perform religious ceremonies, and to afford pastoral care at a Protestant church.
definitions.example: The minister said a prayer on behalf of the entire congregation.

definitions.definition: A politician who heads a ministry (national or regional government department for public service).
definitions.example: He was newly appointed to be Minister of the Interior.

definitions.definition: At a diplomacy, the rank of diplomat directly below ambassador.
A servant; a subordinate; an officer or assistant of inferior rank; hence, an agent, an instrument.
To attend to (the needs of); to tend; to take care (of); to give aid; to give service.
To function as a clergyman or as the officiant in church worship
To afford, to give, to supply.
A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.
A sign or symbol used to indicate a direction (e.g. →).
A directed edge.
A dart.
The -> symbol, which has specific meanings in various programming languages.
The inflorescence or tassel of a mature sugar cane plant.
To move swiftly and directly (like an arrow)
To let fly swiftly and directly
(of a sugar cane plant) To develop an inflorescence.
To navigate using the arrow keys.
definitions.example: Arrow left until you reach the start of the text you want to delete.

definitions.definition: (often followed by “for”) To make an apology or excuse; to acknowledge some fault or offense, with expression of regret for it, by way of amends
definitions.example: My correspondent apologized for not answering my letter.

definitions.definition: To express regret that a certain event has occurred.
To make an apologia or defense; to act as apologist.
That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.
definitions.example: moral absolutes

definitions.definition: Anything that is absolute.
In a plane, the two imaginary circular points at infinity; in space of three dimensions, the imaginary circle at infinity.
(usually capitalized) A realm which exists without reference to anything else; that which can be imagined purely by itself; absolute ego.
(usually capitalized) The unity of spirit and nature; God.
(usually capitalized) The whole of reality; the totality to which everything is reduced.
A concentrated natural flower oil, used for perfumes; an alcoholic extract of a concrete.
Free of restrictions, limitations, qualifications or conditions; unconditional.
definitions.example: 1658, Samuel Hoard, God[']s Love to Mankind, Manifested, by disprooving his absolute decree for their damnation

definitions.definition: Free from imperfection, perfect, complete; especially, perfectly embodying a quality in its essential characteristics or to its highest degree.
definitions.example: absolute purity, absolute liberty

definitions.definition: Pure, free from mixture or adulteration; unmixed.
definitions.example: absolute alcohol

definitions.definition: Complete, utter, outright; unmitigated, not qualified or diminished in any way.
definitions.example: When caught, he told an absolute lie.   an absolute denial of all charges

definitions.definition: Positive, certain; unquestionable.
Certain; free from doubt or uncertainty (e.g. a person, opinion or prediction).
Fundamental, ultimate, intrinsic; not relative; independent of references or relations to other things or standards.
definitions.example: Absolute rights and duties are such as pertain to man in a state of nature as contradistinguished from relative rights and duties, or such as pertain to him in his social relations.

definitions.definition: Independent of arbitrary units of measurement, standards, or properties; not comparative or relative.
definitions.example: absolute velocity, absolute motion, absolute position

definitions.definition: (grammar) Not immediately dependent on the other parts of the sentence; not in a syntactical relation with other parts of a text, or qualifying the text as a whole rather than any single word in it, like "it being over" in "it being over, she left".
As measured using an absolute value.
definitions.example: absolute deviation

definitions.definition: Indicating an expression that is true for all real numbers, or of all values of the variable; unconditional.
Pertaining to a grading system based on the knowledge of the individual and not on the comparative knowledge of the group of students.
Independent of (references to) other arts; expressing things (beauty, ideas, etc) only in one art.
definitions.example: absolute music

definitions.definition: Absolved; free.
An entrance capable of being blocked by use of a gate.
Any point that represents the beginning of a transition from one place or phase to another.
A point at which freight moving from one territory to another is interchanged between transportation lines.

(digital communications) In wireless internet, an access point with additional software capabilities such as providing NAT and DHCP, which may also provide VPN support, roaming, firewalls, various levels of security, etc.
To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).
To commit oneself (to an obligation, activity etc.).
definitions.example: He undertook to exercise more in future.

definitions.definition: To overtake on the wrong side.
definitions.example: I hate people who try and undertake on the motorway.

definitions.definition: To pledge; to assert, assure; to dare say.
To take by trickery; to trap, to seize upon.
To assume, as a character; to take on.
To engage with; to attack, take on in a fight.
To have knowledge of; to hear.
To have or take charge of.
A diminutive of the pale, usually appearing in pairs on either side of a pale.
To support, to back, to give one's approval to, especially officially or by signature.
To write one's signature on the back of a cheque, or other negotiable instrument, when transferring it to a third party, or cashing it.
To give an endorsement.

Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
Very elevated; extending or being far above a base; tall; lofty.
definitions.example: The balloon rose high in the sky.   The wall was high.   a high mountain

definitions.definition: Relatively elevated; rising or raised above the average or normal level from which elevation is measured.
Having a specified elevation or height; tall.
definitions.example: three feet high   three Mount Everests high

definitions.definition: Elevated in status, esteem, prestige; exalted in rank, station, or character.
definitions.example: The oldest of the elves' royal family still conversed in High Elvish.

definitions.definition: Of great importance and consequence: grave (if negative) or solemn (if positive).
definitions.example: high crimes, the high festival of the sun

definitions.definition: Consummate; advanced (e.g. in development) to the utmost extent or culmination, or possessing a quality in its supreme degree, at its zenith.
definitions.example: high (i.e. intense) heat; high (i.e. full or quite) noon; high (i.e. rich or spicy) seasoning; high (i.e. complete) pleasure; high (i.e. deep or vivid) colour; high (i.e. extensive, thorough) scholarship; high tide; high [tourism] season; the High Middle Ages

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Remote in distance or time.
definitions.example: high latitude, high antiquity

definitions.definition: (in several set phrases) Very traditionalist and conservative, especially in favoring older ways of doing things; see e.g. high church, High Tory.
Elevated in mood; marked by great merriment, excitement, etc.
definitions.example: in high spirits

definitions.definition: (of a lifestyle) Luxurious; rich.
definitions.example: high living, the high life

definitions.definition: Lofty, often to the point of arrogant, haughty, boastful, proud.
definitions.example: a high tone

definitions.definition: (with "on" or "about") Keen, enthused.
(of a body of water) With tall waves.
Large, great (in amount or quantity, value, force, energy, etc).
definitions.example: My bank charges me a high interest rate.   I was running a high temperature and had high cholesterol.   high voltage   high prices   high winds   a high number

definitions.definition: (acoustics) Acute or shrill in pitch, due to being of greater frequency, i.e. produced by more rapid vibrations (wave oscillations).
definitions.example: The note was too high for her to sing.

definitions.definition: Made with some part of the tongue positioned high in the mouth, relatively close to the palate.
Greater in value than other cards, denominations, suits, etc.
(of meat, especially venison) Strong-scented; slightly tainted/spoiled; beginning to decompose.
definitions.example: Epicures do not cook game before it is high.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated; under the influence of a mood-altering drug, formerly usually alcohol, but now (from the mid-20th century) usually not alcohol but rather marijuana, cocaine, heroin, etc.
(of a sailing ship) Near, in its direction of travel, to the (direction of the) wind.
In or to an elevated position.
definitions.example: How high above land did you fly?

definitions.definition: In or at a great value.
definitions.example: Costs have grown higher this year again.

definitions.definition: At a pitch of great frequency.
definitions.example: I certainly can't sing that high.

definitions.definition: A national school-leaving examination and university entrance qualification.
To make higher; to raise or increase in amount or quantity.
To ascend.
To restrain within boundaries; to limit; to confine
definitions.example: After suffering diahrroea, the patient was restricted to a diet of rice, cold meat, and yoghurt.

definitions.definition: (specifically) To consider (a function) as defined on a subset of its original domain.
Limited within bounds.
Available only to certain authorized groups of people.
definitions.example: a restricted military document

definitions.definition: Only available to customers who do not belong to racial, ethnic or religious minorities.
definitions.example: This hotel is restricted: no Blacks.

definitions.definition: A chasm or fissure.
definitions.example: My marriage is in trouble: the fight created a rift between us and we can't reconnect.

definitions.definition: A break in the clouds, fog, mist etc., which allows light through.
A shallow place in a stream; a ford.
To form a rift; to split open.
To cleave; to rive; to split.
definitions.example: to rift an oak

definitions.definition: A person seeking refuge in a foreign country out of fear of political persecution or the prospect of such persecution in their home country, i.e., a person seeking political asylum.
A person seeking refuge due to a natural disaster, war, etc.
A person formally granted political or economic asylum by a country other than their home country.
(by extension) A person who flees one place or institution for another.
To convey (slaves) away from the advance of the federal forces.
A party bringing a suit in civil law against a defendant; accusers.
One unknown or in disguise, or under an assumed character or name.
The assumption of disguise or of a feigned character; the state of being in disguise or not recognized.
Without being known; in disguise; in an assumed character, or under an assumed title.
Without revealing one's identity.
An advance into enemy territory, an incursion, an attempted invasion
(usually plural) progress made toward accomplishing a goal or solving a problem
To make an inroad into; to invade.
The path an object takes as it moves.
The path of a body as it travels through space.
The ordered set of intermediate states assumed by a dynamical system as a result of time evolution.
A course of development, such as that of a war or career.
Endowed with one or more talents.
Something additional; an extra.
Additional; added to supply what is wanted.
A circular or elliptical path of one object around another object, particularly in astronomy and space travel.
definitions.example: The Moon's orbit around the Earth takes nearly one month to complete.

definitions.definition: A sphere of influence; an area of control.
definitions.example: In the post WWII era, several eastern European countries came into the orbit of the Soviet Union.

definitions.definition: The course of one's usual progression, or the extent of one's typical range.
definitions.example: The convenience store was a heavily travelled point in her daily orbit, as she purchased both cigarettes and lottery tickets there.

definitions.definition: The bony cavity containing the eyeball; the eye socket.
A mathematical function that describes the wave-like behavior of an electron in an atom; area of the highest probability of electron´s occurrence around the atom's nucleus.
A collection of points related by the evolution function of a dynamical system.
The subset of elements of a set X to which a given element can be moved by members of a specified group of transformations that act on X.
A state of increased excitement, activity, or anger.
definitions.example: Dad went into orbit when I told him that I'd crashed the car.

definitions.definition: A path for the ball on the outer edge of the playfield, usually connected so that the ball entering in one end will come out of the other.
To circle or revolve around another object.
definitions.example: The Earth orbits the Sun.

definitions.definition: To move around the general vicinity of something.
definitions.example: The harried mother had a cloud of children orbiting her, begging for sweets.

definitions.definition: To place an object into an orbit around a planet.
definitions.example: A rocket was used to orbit the satellite.

definitions.definition: Belonging to; possessed; proper to. Often marks a possessive determiner as reflexive, referring back to the subject of the clause or sentence.
Not shared
definitions.example: When we move into the new house, the kids will each have their own bedroom.

definitions.definition: Peculiar, domestic.
Not foreign.
To wish, desire.
To instruct (that something be done) in one's will.
To try to make (something) happen by using one's will (intention).
definitions.example: All the fans were willing their team to win the game.

definitions.definition: To bequeath (something) to someone in one's will (legal document).
definitions.example: He willed his stamp collection to the local museum.

definitions.definition: The execution of a will.
Ready to do something that is not (can't be expected as) a matter of course.
definitions.example: If my boyfriend isn't willing to change his drinking habits, I will split up with him.

definitions.definition: A child's nurse.
A grandmother.
A godmother.
A female goat.
To serve as a nanny.
To treat like a nanny's charges; to coddle.
A person who displays nostalgia for something.
Of, having, or relating to nostalgia.
Reminiscent of the speaker's childhood or younger years.
A substance which specifically promotes healing when ingested or consumed in some way.
A treatment or cure.
The study of the cause, diagnosis, prognosis and treatment of disease or illness.
definitions.example: She's studying medicine at university because she wants to be a doctor in the future.

definitions.definition: The profession of physicians, surgeons and related specialisms; those who practice medicine.
Ritual magic used, as by a medicine man, to promote a desired outcome in healing, hunting, warfare etc.
Among the Native Americans, any object supposed to give control over natural or magical forces, to act as a protective charm, or to cause healing.
Black magic, superstition.
A philter or love potion.
A physician.
Recreational drugs, especially alcoholic drinks.
To treat with medicine.
Capable of being understood; comprehensible.
Capable of being accepted or excused under the circumstances.
One who is stooped or hunched over.
A deformed upper spinal column in the shape of a hump in the back.
A person with kyphosis, a spinal deformity that causes a hunched over appearance.
Something in between hardware and software. Like software, it is created from source code, but it is closely tied to the hardware it runs on.
Software intended for such embedded computer applications.
(often in the plural) A very small piece of plush material.
definitions.example: You've got a fuzzy on your coat.

definitions.definition: Something covered with fuzz or hair, as an animal or plush toy.
A person, especially a college student, interested in humanities or social sciences, as opposed to one interested in mathematics, science, or engineering.
A soldier with the rank of private.
Covered with fuzz or a large number of tiny loose fibres like a carpet or many stuffed animals
Vague or imprecise.
definitions.example: My recollection of that event is fuzzy.

definitions.definition: Not clear; unfocused.
definitions.example: I finally threw out a large stack of fuzzy photos.

definitions.definition: Having an innately kind disposition or character.
The act of signing up or registering for something.
definitions.example: Complete the registration process.

definitions.definition: That which registers or makes something official, e.g. the form or paper that registers.
definitions.example: Did you submit your car registration yet?

definitions.definition: Alignment, e.g. of colors or other elements in a printing process.
The location where guests register, especially with a hotel.
definitions.example: The elevators are just past registration.

definitions.definition: The art of selecting and combining the stops or registers of an organ.
The act of implicating.
The state of being implicated.
(usually in the plural) A possible effect or result of a decision or action.
definitions.example: There are serious implications for the environment of such reforms.

definitions.definition: An implying, or that which is implied, but not expressed; an inference, or something which may fairly be understood, though not expressed in words.
The connective in propositional calculus that, when joining two predicates A and B in that order, has the meaning "if A is true, then B is true".
Logical consequence.
The act of imposing taxes and the fact of being taxed.
A particular system of taxing people or companies
The revenue gained from taxes

To behave as a jaywalker; to violate pedestrian traffic regulations by crossing a street away from a designated crossing or to walk in the part of the street intended for vehicles rather than on the sidewalk.
A legless reptile of the sub-order Serpentes with a long, thin body and a fork-shaped tongue.
A treacherous person.
Somebody who acts deceitfully for social gain.
A tool for unclogging plumbing.
A tool to aid cable pulling.
A flavoured jube (confectionary) in the shape of a snake.
Trouser snake; the penis.
A series of Bézier curves.
The seventh Lenormand card.
To follow or move in a winding route.
definitions.example: The path snaked through the forest.

definitions.definition: To steal slyly.
definitions.example: He snaked my DVD!

definitions.definition: To clean using a plumbing snake.
To drag or draw, as a snake from a hole; often with out.
To wind round spirally, as a large rope with a smaller, or with cord, the small rope lying in the spaces between the strands of the large one; to worm.
A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.
A drudge; one who labors or is obliged (e.g. by prior contract) to labor like a slave with limited rights, e.g. an indentured servant.
An abject person.
One who has no power of resistance (to something), one who surrenders to or is under the domination (of something).
definitions.example: a slave to passion, to strong drink, or to ambition

definitions.definition: (BDSM) A submissive partner in a BDSM relationship who (consensually) submits to (sexually and/or personally) serving one or more masters or mistresses.
A sex slave, a person who is forced against their will to perform, for another person or group, sexual acts on a regular or continuing basis.
A device (such as a secondary flash or hard drive) that is subject to the control of another (a master).
To work as a slaver, to enslave people.
To work hard.
definitions.example: I was slaving all day over a hot stove.

definitions.definition: To place a device under the control of another.
definitions.example: to slave a hard disk

definitions.definition: The right to use or enjoy something exclusively or before anyone else.
definitions.example: Dibs means I get the hammock.

definitions.definition: To claim a temporary right to (something); to reserve.
Used to claim this right
A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.
Any intermediate object that connects a smaller part to a larger part, the smaller part typically projecting sideways from the larger part.
A short crooked timber, resembling a knee, used as a support.
The cheek or side of an ordnance carriage, supporting the trunnions.
Any of the characters "(", ")", "[", "]", "{", "}", "<" and ">", used in pairs to enclose parenthetic remarks, sections of mathematical expressions, etc.
A printed diagram of games in a tournament.
A prediction of the outcome of games in a tournament, used for betting purposes.
One of several ranges of numbers.
definitions.example: tax bracket, age bracket

definitions.definition: A pair of values that represent the smallest and largest elements of a range.
In artillery, the endangered region between two shell impacts (one long and one short). The next shell fired is likely to hit accurately.
The small curved or angular corner formed by a serif and a stroke in a letter.
(land surveying, 19th century) a mark cut into a stone by land surveyors to secure a bench.
To support by means of mechanical brackets.
To enclose in typographical brackets.
To bound on both sides, to surround, as enclosing with brackets.
definitions.example: I tried to hit the bullseye by first bracketing it with two shots and then splitting the difference with my third, but I missed.

definitions.definition: To place in the same category.
definitions.example: Because the didn't have enough young boys for two full teams, they bracketed the seven-year olds with the eight-year olds.

definitions.definition: To mark distinctly for special treatment.
To set aside, discount, ignore.
To take multiple images of the same subject, using a range of exposure settings, in order to help ensure that a satisfactory image is obtained.

(phenomenology) In the philosophical system of Edmund Husserl and his followers, to set aside metaphysical theories and existential questions concerning what is real in order to focus philosophical attention simply on the actual content of experience.
Outer; located towards the outside
Visible, noticeable
definitions.example: By all outward indications, he's a normal happy child, but if you talk to him, you will soon realize he has some psychological problems.

definitions.definition: Tending to the exterior or outside.
Foreign; not civil or intestine.
definitions.example: an outward war

definitions.definition: Towards the outside; away from the centre.
definitions.example: We are outward bound.

definitions.definition: Outwardly, in outer appearances; publicly.
A room, especially one which is not particularly large, used for manufacturing or other light industrial work.
A brief, intensive course of education for a small group, emphasizing interaction and practical problem solving.
An academic conference.
To help a playwright revise a draft of (a play) by rehearsing it with actors and critiquing the results.
To work on or revise something, especially collaboratively, in a workshop.
To improve through collaboration.
The action of rubbing, kneading or hitting someone's body, to help the person relax, prepare for muscular action (as in contact sports) or to relieve aches.
definitions.example: Having a massage can have many beneficial effects.

definitions.definition: To rub and knead (someone's body or a part of a body), to perform a massage on (somebody).
To manipulate (data, a document etc.) to make it more presentable or more convenient to work with.
To falsify (data or accounts).
A tower or other structure exhibiting a light or lights to warn or guide sailors.
To depart; to go to another place.
definitions.example: At 9 o'clock sharp he awayed to bed.

definitions.definition: Not here, gone, absent, unavailable, traveling; on vacation.
definitions.example: The master is away from home.

definitions.definition: At a specified distance in space, time, or figuratively.
definitions.example: He's miles away by now.

definitions.definition: Not on one's home territory.
definitions.example: Next, they are playing away in Dallas.

definitions.definition: (following the noun modified) Out.
definitions.example: Two men away in the bottom of the ninth.

definitions.definition: From a place, hence.
definitions.example: He went away on vacation.

definitions.definition: Aside; off; in another direction.
definitions.example: I tried to approach him, but he turned away.

definitions.definition: Aside, so as to discard something.
definitions.example: throw away, chuck away, toss away

definitions.definition: At a stated distance in time or space.
definitions.example: Christmas is only two weeks away.

definitions.definition: In or to something's usual or proper storage place.
definitions.example: I'll dry the dishes and you put them away.

definitions.definition: In or to a secure or out-of-the-way place.
definitions.example: He was shut away in the castle tower for six months.

definitions.definition: From a state or condition of being; out of existence.
definitions.example: fade away, die away

definitions.definition: So as to remove or use up something.
definitions.example: Please wipe away this spilled drink.

definitions.definition: (as imperative, by ellipsis) Come away; go away; take away.
definitions.example: Away! Be gone! And don't let me see you round here again!

definitions.definition: On; in continuance; without intermission or delay.
definitions.example: She's been in her room all day, working away at her computer.

definitions.definition: Without restraint.
definitions.example: I saw her whaling away at her detractors.

definitions.definition: Come on!; go on!
A small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched from a grenade launcher.
A pomegranate.
A charge similar to a fireball, and made of a disc-shaped bomb shell, but with only one set of flames at the top.
An unattractive girl.
To use grenade(s) upon.
A sound produced by the vocal cords with relatively little restriction of the oral cavity, forming the prominent sound of a syllable.
A letter representing the sound of vowel; in English, the vowels are a, e, i, o, u, and sometimes y.
To defeat, to overcome.
Defeated.
To make a sound from the vocal chords without pronouncing any real words, with one's lips closed.
definitions.example: We are humming happily along with the music.

definitions.definition: To express by humming.
definitions.example: The team ominously hummed “We shall overcome” as they came back onto the field after the break.

definitions.definition: To drone like certain insects naturally do in motion, or sounding similarly
To buzz, be busily active like a beehive
definitions.example: The streets were humming with activity.

definitions.definition: To produce low sounds which blend continuously
To reek, smell bad.
definitions.example: This room really hums — have you ever tried spring cleaning, mate?

definitions.definition: To flatter by approving; to cajole; to deceive or impose upon; to humbug.
To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.
definitions.example: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.

definitions.definition: To stammer; to falter in speaking.
To utter with hesitation or to intimate by a reluctant manner.
The time ahead; those moments yet to be experienced.
Something that will happen in moments yet to come.
Goodness in what is yet to come. Something to look forward to.
definitions.example: There is no future in dwelling on the past.

definitions.definition: The likely prospects for or fate of someone or something in time to come.
(grammar) Verb tense used to talk about events that will happen in the future; future tense.
An object that retrieves the value of a promise.
A minor-league prospect.
Short for futures contract.
Some object given by a person and retained in memory of something or someone; something kept for sentimental or nostalgic reasons.
definitions.example: She gave him a lock of hair as a keepsake of their time together.

definitions.definition: Specifically, a type of literary album popular in the nineteenth-century, containing scraps of poetry and prose, and engravings.
An act, or the process, of renovating.
Regeneration.
A safeguard.
To incite; to bring about by urging or encouraging
definitions.example: to instigate a riot

definitions.definition: To goad or urge (a person) forward, especially to wicked actions; to provoke
definitions.example: to instigate someone to a crime

definitions.definition: A newspaper having pages half the dimensions of the standard format.
A newspaper, especially one in this format, that favours stories of a sensational or even fictitious nature over serious news.
A compressed portion of drugs, chemicals, etc.; a tablet.
In the format of a tabloid.
Relating to a tabloid or tabloids.
definitions.example: tabloid journalism

definitions.definition: A soft, white, powdery limestone.
A piece of chalk, or nowadays processed compressed gypsum, that is used for drawing and for writing on a blackboard.
Tailor's chalk.
A white powdery substance used to prevent hands slipping from holds when climbing, sometimes but not always limestone-chalk.
A platoon-sized group of airborne soldiers.
(horseracing) The favorite in a sporting event.
The prediction that there will be no upsets, and the favored competitor will win.
To apply chalk to anything, such as the tip of a billiard cue.
To record something, as on a blackboard, using chalk.
To use powdered chalk to mark the lines on a playing field.
To record a score or event, as if on a chalkboard.
To manure (land) with chalk.
To make white, as if with chalk; to make pale; to bleach.
A room, often in a school, where classes take place
Having a tendency to cling.
definitions.example: a clingy minidress

definitions.definition: (usually derogatory) Pathetically attached to, or possessive of someone, usually a significant other.
definitions.example: a clingy ex-girlfriend who won't stop calling

definitions.definition: Of, or having to do with science.
Having the quality of being derived from, or consistent with, the scientific method.
In accord with procedures, methods, conduct and accepted conventions of modern science.
A small domed cap that covers the area from the forehead to just above the back of the neck.
A yarmulke-like hat worn as an element of ghetto fashion.
The calvaria, the top part of the skull, covering the cranial cavity containing the brain.
Any of several species of flowering plants of the genus Scutellaria, in the Lamiaceae family.
A torture device for compressing the skull.
To tie; to confine by any ligature.
To cohere or stick together in a mass.
definitions.example: Just to make the cheese more binding

definitions.definition: To be restrained from motion, or from customary or natural action, as by friction.
definitions.example: I wish I knew why the sewing machine binds up after I use it for a while.

definitions.definition: To exert a binding or restraining influence.
definitions.example: These are the ties that bind.

definitions.definition: To tie or fasten tightly together, with a cord, band, ligature, chain, etc.
definitions.example: to bind grain in bundles  to bind a prisoner

definitions.definition: To confine, restrain, or hold by physical force or influence of any kind.
definitions.example: Frost binds the earth.

definitions.definition: To couple.
To oblige, restrain, or hold, by authority, law, duty, promise, vow, affection, or other social tie.
definitions.example: to bind the conscience  to bind by kindness  bound by affection  commerce binds nations to each other

definitions.definition: To put (a person) under definite legal obligations, especially, under the obligation of a bond or covenant.
To place under legal obligation to serve.
definitions.example: to bind an apprentice  bound out to service

definitions.definition: To protect or strengthen by applying a band or binding, as the edge of a carpet or garment.
To make fast (a thing) about or upon something, as by tying; to encircle with something.
definitions.example: to bind a belt about one  to bind a compress upon a wound

definitions.definition: To cover, as with a bandage.
definitions.example: to bind up a wound

definitions.definition: To prevent or restrain from customary or natural action, as by producing constipation.
definitions.example: Certain drugs bind the bowels.

definitions.definition: To put together in a cover, as of books.
definitions.example: The three novels were bound together.

definitions.definition: To make two or more elements stick together.
To associate an identifier with a value; to associate a variable name, method name, etc. with the content of a storage location.
To complain; to whine about something.
(with infinitive) Obliged (to).
definitions.example: You are not legally bound to reply.

definitions.definition: (of a morpheme) That cannot stand alone as a free word.
(of a variable) Constrained by a quantifier.
Constipated; costive.
Confined or restricted to a certain place; e.g. railbound.
Unable to move in certain conditions; e.g. snowbound.
A person who arranges the details of a public event.
A hand-held micro-computer that will perform specific tasks; can be used as an electronic diary, alarm clock, recorder of memos and notes, a portable database etc.
A group of cells that, together with the evocator, control differentiation in the embryo; the inductor
The action of opposing or of being in conflict.
An opposite or contrasting position.
The apparent relative position of two celestial bodies when one is at an angle of 180 degrees from the other as seen from the Earth.
A political party or movement opposed to the party or government in power.
In United States intellectual property law, a proceeding in which an interested party seeks to prevent the registration of a trademark or patent.
A position in which the player on the move must yield with his king allowing his opponent to advance with his own king.
The difference of quantity or quality between two propositions having the same subject and predicate.

Any of the days of a week on which work is done. The five workdays in many countries are usually Monday to Friday (and are defined as such in official and legal usage even though many people work on weekends).
definitions.example: It will take five workdays to process your application.

definitions.definition: That part of a day in which work is done.
definitions.example: My workday is 8 hours.

definitions.definition: The action of naturalizing somebody; act of granting citizenship.
The admission or adoption of foreign words or customs into general use.
The introduction and establishment of an animal or plant into a place where it is not indigenous.
Matter which may be shaped or manipulated, particularly in making something.
definitions.example: Asphalt, composed of oil and sand, is a widely used material for roads.

definitions.definition: Text written for a specific purpose.
definitions.example: We were a warm-up act at the time; we didn't have enough original material to headline.

definitions.definition: A sample or specimens for study.
Cloth to be made into a garment. Fabric.
definitions.example: You'll need about a yard of material to make this.

definitions.definition: The people collectively who are qualified for a certain position or activity.
definitions.example: He is not the only one. I believe we have lots of presidential material in various public offices.

definitions.definition: Related data of various kinds, especially if collected as the basis for a document or book.
The substance that something is made or composed of.
All of a player's pieces and pawns on the chessboard.
To form from matter; to materialize.
A small sponge cake, shaped approximately like a finger.
definitions.example: Ladyfingers soaked in liqueur are the base of tiramisu, but I suppose you could just cut up a sponge cake instead.

definitions.definition: A type of small banana.
A variety of small firecracker.
definitions.example: Grandpa would give each of the kids a strip of ladyfingers to light on New Year's Day.

definitions.definition: The edible immature mucilaginous seed pod (properly, capsule) of the Abelmoschus esculentus.
The flowering mallow plant Abelmoschus esculentus itself, now commonly grown in the tropics and warmer parts of the temperate zones.
A place of safety.
The protection, physical and legal, afforded by such a place.
A place of protection or restraint for one or more classes of the disadvantaged, especially the mentally ill.
An angry glare or stare.
definitions.example: She sure has an awful glower on her face.

definitions.definition: To look or stare with anger.
That which glows or emits light.
A person who is recognized by a university as having completed the requirements of a degree studied at the institution.
definitions.example: If the government wants graduates to stay in the country they should offer more incentives.

definitions.definition: A person who is recognized by a high school as having completed the requirements of a course of study at the school.
A person who is recognized as having completed any level of education.
A graduated (marked) cup or other container, thus fit for measuring.
To be recognized by a school or university as having completed the requirements of a degree studied at the institution.
definitions.example: The man graduated in 1967.

definitions.definition: To be certified as having earned a degree from; to graduate from (an institution).
definitions.example: Trisha graduated college.

definitions.definition: To certify (a student) as having earned a degree
definitions.example: Indiana University graduated the student.

definitions.definition: To mark (something) with degrees; to divide into regular steps or intervals, as the scale of a thermometer, a scheme of punishment or rewards, etc.
To change gradually.
definitions.example: sandstone which graduates into gneiss; carnelian sometimes graduates into quartz

definitions.definition: To prepare gradually; to arrange, temper, or modify by degrees or to a certain degree; to determine the degrees of.
definitions.example: to graduate the heat of an oven

definitions.definition: To bring to a certain degree of consistency, by evaporation, as a fluid.
To taper, as the tail of certain birds.
Graduated, arranged by degrees
Holding an academic degree
Relating to an academic degree
A small piece of cloth inserted in a garment, for the purpose of strengthening some part or giving it a tapering enlargement cf. godet.
A small piece of mail, providing some protection where two plates of armor are joined, usually at the elbows, under the shoulders, and behind the knees.

(machinery) A kind of bracket, or angular piece of iron, fastened in the angles of a structure to give strength or stiffness; especially, the part joining the barrel and the fire box of a locomotive boiler.
An abatement or mark of dishonor in a coat of arms, resembling a gusset.
(roofing) A large flat metal piece wider than the valley to help prevent build-up at the base of the valley, either from debris or ice dam formations.
To make with a gusset; to sew a gusset into.
An area dominated by grass or grasslike vegetation.
A band passed under the belly of an animal, which holds a saddle or a harness saddle in place.
The part of an animal around which the girth fits.
One's waistline circumference, most often a large one.
A small horizontal brace or girder.
The distance measured around an object.
The length of the shortest cycle in a graph.
To bind as if with a girth or band.
A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
An event that is not, or cannot be, foreseen.
The thing risked; especially, something sent to sea in trade.
A fabulous winged monster, ravenous and filthy, having the face of a woman and the body of a vulture.
A shrewish woman.
One who is rapacious or ravenous; an extortioner.
The European moor buzzard or marsh harrier (Circus aeruginosus).
A large and powerful double-crested, short-winged American eagle (Harpia harpyja).
Often plural: a belief or custom common to members of a culture or society.
Potos flavus, a carnivorous mammal of Central America and South America with a long, prehensile tail, related to the raccoon.
To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence
definitions.example: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.

definitions.definition: To become aware of (a fact or situation, especially of something that has been true for a long time).
definitions.example: He realized that he had left his umbrella on the train.

definitions.definition: To cause to seem real; to sense vividly or strongly; to make one's own in thought or experience.
To acquire as an actual possession; to obtain as the result of plans and efforts; to gain; to get
definitions.example: to realize large profits from a speculation

definitions.definition: To convert any kind of property into money, especially property representing investments, such as shares, bonds, etc.
definitions.example: By realizing the company's assets, the liquidator was able to return most of the shareholders' investments.

definitions.definition: To convert into real property; to make real estate of.
To turn an abstract linguistic object into actual language, especially said of a phoneme's conversion into speech sound.
definitions.example: The southern /v/ is realized as the voiced approximant [ʋ].

definitions.definition: Either of two lean, white marine food fishes, of the genus Pollachius, in the cod family.
To fish for pollock.
One who pays a fee (rent) in return for the use of land, buildings, or other property owned by others.
One who has possession of any place.
One who holds a property by any kind of right, including ownership.
Any of a number of customers serviced through the same instance of an application.
To hold as, or be, a tenant.
To inhabit.
The thin, horny, transparent plate covering the upper surface of the end of a toe.
To fasten two pieces of lumber together by applying nails or screws into both boards at an angle.

A musical art form rooted in West African cultural and musical expression and in the African American blues tradition, with diverse influences over time, commonly characterized by blue notes, syncopation, swing, call and response, polyrhythms and improvisation.
Energy, excitement, excitability.
The substance or makeup of a thing.

definitions.definition: Unspecified thing(s).
definitions.example: I'm just going down to the shops and jazz.

definitions.definition: (with positive terms) Something of excellent quality, the genuine article.
definitions.example: That show was the jazz!

definitions.definition: Nonsense.
definitions.example: Stop talking jazz.

definitions.definition: Semen, jizz.
To destroy.
definitions.example: You’ve gone and jazzed it now!

definitions.definition: To play (jazz music).
To dance to the tunes of jazz music.
To enliven, brighten up, make more colourful or exciting; excite
To complicate.

definitions.definition: To have sex for money, to prostitute oneself.
To move (around/about) in a lively or frivolous manner; to fool around.
To distract or pester.
definitions.example: Stop jazzing me!

definitions.definition: To ejaculate.
The act of taking care or taking charge of something.
A disguise or covering up.
The act of disguising.
The use of natural or artificial material on personnel, objects, or tactical positions with the aim of confusing, misleading, or evading the enemy.
A pattern on clothing consisting of irregularly shaped patches that are either greenish/brownish, brownish/whitish, or bluish/whitish, as used by ground combat forces.
Resemblance of an organism to its surroundings for avoiding detection.
Clothes made from camouflage fabric, for concealment in combat or hunting.
To hide or disguise something by covering it up or changing the way it looks.
To twist or whirl, as an eddy.

definitions.definition: To be arranged in a twist, spiral or whorl.
To circulate.
A pattern or motion that swirls.
That swirls
A thin, flat, circular plate or similar object.

definitions.definition: Something resembling a disk.

definitions.definition: An intervertebral disc
A vinyl phonograph/gramophone record.
definitions.example: Turn the disk over, after it has finished.

definitions.definition: A disc - either a CD-ROM, an audio CD, a DVD or similar removable storage medium.
definitions.example: She burned some disks yesterday to back up her computer.

definitions.definition: A type of harrow.
A ring- or cup-shaped enlargement of the flower receptacle or ovary that bears nectar or, less commonly, the stamens.
To harrow
A removable disk used for storing digital data, measuring between 2 and 8 inches diagonally and storing between 80 KB and 240 MB.
As it was in the beginning.
definitions.example: Originally, this paper was white, but over the years it has yellowed with age.

definitions.definition: In an original manner.
definitions.example: They had to think originally to come up with those creative ideas.

definitions.definition: A high military officer in a warlike nation.
A local ruler or bandit leader usually where the government is weak.
Receiving from someone's care what one has earlier entrusted to them. Usually refers to money.
A method of birth control which consists of removing the penis from the vagina before ejaculation.
A type of metabolic shock the body undergoes when a substance, usually a toxin such as heroin, to which a patient is dependent is withheld. Sometimes used with the substance as modifier.

definitions.definition: An act of withdrawing.
The sum of money taken from a bank account.
Something that is marginal.
A constituency won with a small margin.
Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).
definitions.example: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.

definitions.definition: Determined by a small margin; having a salient characteristic determined by a small margin.
Pertaining to changes resulting from a unit increase in production or consumption of a good.
Having much learning, knowledgeable, erudite; highly educated.
A courteous description used in various ways to refer to lawyers or judges, including:
Scholarly (exhibiting scholarship)
A large organ in the body that stores and metabolizes nutrients, destroys toxins and produces bile. It is responsible for thousands of biochemical reactions.

definitions.definition: This organ, as taken from animals used as food.

definitions.definition: A dark brown colour, tinted with red and gray, like the colour of liver.
Someone who lives (usually in a specified way).
The left side or direction.
The ensemble of left-wing political parties. Those holding left-wing views as a group.

A punch delivered with the left fist.
A wave breaking from left to right (viewed from the shore).
The opposite of right; toward the west when one is facing north.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the political left.
On the left side.
Towards the left side.

definitions.definition: Towards the political left.
